data.title: First Citizens, Synovus nab Buy rates as Deutsche sees a bright 2025 for banks
data.description:
Deutsche Bank Securities started coverage on First Citizens BancShares (NASDAQ:FCNCA) and Synovus Financial (NYSE:SNV) with Buy ratings in a recent note as analyst Bernard von-Gizycki sees an encouraging, pro-growth landscape for banks next year.

The bullish view is "supported by expectations post the US elections driven by continued macro growth, a pro-growth/business environment, deregulation and an opening up of the M&A markets," Gizycki wrote in a note to clients over the weekend.

Focused on Snyovus (NYSE:SNV), the analyst said he expects the lender's loan activity to pick up starting in the second half of 2025 and accelerate into 2026. Earnings growth, in turn, is seen growing above peers for both FY2025 and FY2026.

What's more, he added, the company's "balance sheet optimization has peaked and mgmt is accelerating investments to drive long term value in growing loans, deposits and fee income in high value areas." SNV has strong franchise value including a favorable geographic footprint which should warrant a premium valuation in a pick up/opening up of the M&A environment."

Specifically on First Citizens (NASDAQ:FCNCA), Gizycki's bull thesis centers on potential loan growth from a pick-up in capital markets activity, as well as strong capital levels supporting share buybacks and balance sheet flexibility to navigate different interest-rate backdrops.

(SNV) slipped 0.3% minutes after the opening bell, while (FCNCA) gained 0.5%.

data.title: LTC Properties names co-CEOs, CFO
data.description:
• LTC Properties (NYSE:LTC) has announced some leadership updates, with Pam Kessler and Clint Malin to take the helm as co-CEOs and Cece Chikhale promoted to chief financial officer.
  • Kessler was previously serving as co-president alongside Malin since 2020. They succeed Wendy Simpson, who was named executive chairman of board of directors. Simpson, 75, had been serving as LTC’s chief executive officer since 2007.
  • Chikhale, meanwhile, has been LTC’s chief accounting officer since 2020 and has been controller and treasurer since 2007.

data.title: SELLAS reports positive survival data from mid-stage trial of its leukemia treatment
data.description:
• SELLAS Life Sciences Group (NASDAQ:SLS) announced additional data from the expansion cohorts in the mid-stage study of SLS009, a highly selective CDK9 inhibitor, in relapsed/refractory acute myeloid leukemia.
  • The median overall survival has not yet reached, it now exceeds 7.7. months at latest follow-up in the 30 mg BIW cohort in patients relapsed or refractory to venetoclax-based regimens
  • The overall response rate of 56% achieved to date in patient with acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia related changes prospectively enrolled in two expansion cohorts.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Robinhood, Schwab raised to Overweight at Barclays as market conditions brighten
data.description:
Barclays upgraded Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) and Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) to Overweight from Equal Weight for different reasons as market conditions look more constructive heading into 2025, especially for alternative asset managers and brokers.

For Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD), Barclays analyst Benjamin Budish sees a "uniquely differentiated growth opportunity set and an increasingly demonstrated ability to execute." The company should also directly benefit from crypto. "We see conditions that have benefitted the company over the course of FY24 (higher-for-longer rates; constructive equity markets; etc.) as set to continue or improve (crypto in particular)," the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

The Overweight rating for HOOD contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and aligns with the average SA Analyst rating and average Wall Street rating, both at Buy.

In Schwab's (NYSE:SCHW) case, the company's medium-term EPS growth trajectory should one of the “easiest” in Budish's coverage, "with cash sorting and M&A-related attrition likely coming to an end, supporting meaningful NII and NIM improvement over the next few years."

"After a long period of rising rates putting pressure on customer cash balances, the cash realignment dynamic that begun around the end of 2022 appears to have finally eased," he wrote.

The Overweight rating for Schwab (SCHW) aligns with the SA Quant rating of Strong Buy and the average Wall Street rating of Buy, but differs from the average SA Analyst rating of Hold.

Schwab (SCHW) stock gained 1.3%, and Robinhood (HOOD) stock slipped 0.6% in Monday premarket trading.

data.title: Ballard Power to supply 8 MW of fuel cell engines for California passenger rail
data.description:
Ballard Power Systems (NASDAQ:BLDP) +2.4% pre-market Monday after saying it signed a multi-year supply agreement with Stadler US to supply 8 MW of fuel cell engines to power trains for the California Department of Transportation; financial terms were not disclosed.

Ballard Power (BLDP) said it will supply FCmove-HD+ engines to be integrated into Stadler's line of zero-emission passenger trains, the FLIRT H2.

The order supports Caltrans' purchase of 10 FLIRT H2 trains, with an option for an additional 19 trains, to strengthen California's zero-emission passenger rail capabilities.

Ballard Power (BLDP) already had integrated its fuel cell engines into the FLIRT H2, which became the first Federal Railroad Administration compliant hydrogen fuel cell powered passenger train demonstrated in the U.S.

data.title: Forum Energy Technologies announces $75M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Forum Energy Technologies (NYSE:FET) board has authorized a $75M share repurchase program
  • In Q4, FET completed sales of two manufacturing facilities located in Texas. Net proceeds totaled ~$20M. 
  • FET shares surged over 17% in premarket trading on Monday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Guardian Exploration signs LOI for acquisition of Sundog gold project
data.description:
• Guardian Exploration (TSXV:GX:CA) on Monday said it has entered into a non-binding letter of intent with New Break Resources for the purchase of New Break’s 100% interest in the mineral rights and exploration data associated with the 9,415 hectare Sundog gold project located in Kivalliq Region, Nunavut.
  • In consideration, upon closing, Guardian will issue 5M common shares in the share capital of the company to New Break and make a cash payment of $75,000.
  • Guardian is expected to assume all the obligations under the MEA including the annual exploration expenditure requirements and annual rent payments due to Nunavut Tunngavik Incorporated.
  • In addition, New Break will retain a 20% interest in the Sundog project, carried through to production.
  • The transaction is expected to close no later than February 28, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Macy's defends itself as a notable activist investor digs in
data.description:
Macy's (NYSE:M) responded early on Monday to pressure from activist investor Barington Capital to make significant changes.

The company said its board and management team are committed to delivering sustainable, profitable growth and driving shareholder value.

"We have consistently demonstrated open-mindedness, including with respect to regularly reviewing the company’s strategy and capital allocation framework and exploring all paths to enhance value," read a statement.

Looking ahead, Macy's (M) said it is confident in its Bold New Chapter strategy, which it highlighted continues to gain traction across all three of its pillars.

Meanwhile, Barington Capital Chairman James Mitarotonda said the firm invested in Macy's (M) because it believes shares are mispriced relative to the upside potential and the compelling value of the company’s owned real estate assets. However, the activist firm is concerned with Macy's (M) large capital expenditure programs in particular.

Shares of Macy's (M) showed a 3.3% gain in premarket action on Monday.

data.title: LM Funding America acquires assets of 15 MW mining site in Oklahoma
data.description:
• LM Funding America (NASDAQ:LMFA) said it has acquired assets of a 15 MW mining site in Oklahoma from Tech Infrastructure JV (JV) for $7.3 million.
  • The firm initially funded the JV’s site development with loans and accrued interest totaling $3.8 million.
  • At close, the company applied the credit to the purchase price and paid ~$1M in cash.
  • The company escrowed an additional $2.5 million of the purchase price, and expects to utilize the full 15MW of $0.04 power for its current mining fleet.
  • In Q1 2025, the firm is expected to have 562 petahash combined mining capacity at the Oklahoma site.

data.title: Nokia upgraded on telco recovering, larger partnership with Microsoft: JP Morgan
data.description:
Nokia (NYSE:NOK) was upgraded to Overweight from Neutral by J.P. Morgan due in part to a recovery in telecommunications spending in 2025 and the Finnish company's newly expanded partnership with Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT).

"Looking into '25, JPMC expects that a small recovery in telco spending is likely due to the end of the US telco inventory correction and the next wave of 5G spending in India," said J.P. Morgan analyst Sandeep Deshpande, in an investor note. "Nokia should also grow in the enterprise market, E.G. the recent expansion of its relationship with Microsoft Cloud."

Last month, Nokia expanded its multi-year agreement to supply Microsoft Azure datacenter networks, strengthening its role as a strategic supplier for Microsoft's cloud infrastructure. The partnership will grow Nokia's global footprint to more than 30 countries.

J.P. Morgan increased its price target on the stock to €6.05 from €4, or to $6.40 from $4.23.

Nokia edged higher during early trading on Monday.

J.P. Morgan also noted that Nokia enters 2025 with beatable earnings estimates.
